data: Entella Tablets VI (254-241 BC) and VII (?C20th AD)
W. T. Loomis, ‘Entella Tablets VI (254-241 BC) and VII (?C20th AD)’, Havard Studies in Classical Philology 96 (1994): 127–60
Entella 6 = C1, renewal of friendship between the Entellinoi and the Assorinoi

Lines 1-3 are roughly centred in the tympanon (although note slight compression at end of line 3); Lines 4-22 occupy the main body of the tablet and the left margin is justified with a margin of c.1 letter space (with the exception of line 4, which is set hard to the left); the right margin is not justified. Line 23 is roughly centred, with larger letters.
First Punic War (264-241 BCE), and commonly attributed to the second half of the war.
Image after cleaning, courtesy of, and copyright of SAET, SNS di Pisa
